questions.question:
How many statements are correct for the growth of organisms?

 All organisms, even the largest, start their life from a single cell.
 Growth and reproduction are characteristics of cells.
 Cycles of growth and division allow a single cell to form a structure consisting of millions of cells.
 Cell division is a very important process in only higher living organisms.
 During the division of a cell, DNA replication and cytoplasmic increase also take place.
 The sequence of events in which cell division, DNA replication and cell growth take place, is called cell cycle.
questions.question_html:
How many statements are correct for the growth of organisms?

  1. All organisms, even the largest, start their life from a single cell.
  2. Growth and reproduction are characteristics of cells.
  3. Cycles of growth and division allow a single cell to form a structure consisting of millions of cells.
  4. Cell division is a very important process in only higher living organisms.
  5. During the division of a cell, DNA replication and cytoplasmic increase also take place.
  6. The sequence of events in which cell division, DNA replication and cell growth take place, is called cell cycle.

questions.question:
Which of the following statements is true regarding cell cycle?

 Cell growth (in terms of cytoplasmic increase) is a continuous process.
 DNA synthesis occurs only during one specific stage in the cell cycle.
 The replicated chromosomes (DNA) are distributed to daughter nuclei.
 Human cells divide once in approximately every 90 minutes.
 Duration of cell cycle can vary from organism to organism and also from cell type to cell type.
 Cell cycle in yeast completed in about 24 hours.

 Some cells in the adult animals do not appear to exhibit division.

questions.question:
Match the entities in Column I with their character in Column II regarding cell cycle:

 
 
 Column I
 Column II
 
 
 (A) G_(1 )- phase
 (i) Cell is preparing for division.
 
 
 (B) G_(2) - phase
 (ii) Actual cell division or mitosis.
 
 
 (C) S - phase
 (iii) Cells in this stage remain metabolically active but no longer proliferate.
 
 
 (D) G_(0 )- phase
 (iv) Proteins are synthesised in preparation for mitosis.
 
 
 (E) Interphase
 (v) DNA becomes 4 C from 2 C.
 
 
 (F) M - phase
 (vi) Cell is metabolically active and continuously grows but does not replicate its DNA.
questions.question_html:
Match the entities in Column I with their character in Column II regarding cell cycle:

Column I Column II
(A) G1 - phase (i) Cell is preparing for division.
(B) G2 - phase (ii) Actual cell division or mitosis.
(C) S - phase (iii) Cells in this stage remain metabolically active but no longer proliferate.
(D) G0 - phase (iv) Proteins are synthesised in preparation for mitosis.
(E) Interphase (v) DNA becomes 4 C from 2 C.
(F) M - phase (vi) Cell is metabolically active and continuously grows but does not replicate its DNA.

questions.question:
Which of the following statements are false for mitosis?

 Mitosis usually results in the production of diploid daughter cells with non-identical genetic complement.
 The growth of multicellular organisms is due to mitosis.
 A very significant contribution of mitosis is cell repair.
 The cells of the upper layer of the epidermis, cells of the lining of the gut and blood cells are being constantly replaced by mitosis.
 Mitotic divisions in the meristematic tissues result in a continuous growth of plants throughout their life.
 This is a specialised kind of cell division that reduces the chromosome number by half and results in the production of haploid daughter cells.
